title: Tavares
common: Family that were owner of Santa Maria de Chãs de Tavares — Portugal. Actually, it is accepted that the family had origin with couple Pedro Viegas and Teresa de Vila Maior, but their [son], Estevão Peres de Taavares, was the first to use the surname ( ; ).

families.name: Tavares (Lamego - Viseu)
history: The 1258 Inquiries cite that Santa Maria de Chãs de Tavares was owned by the family (<htcite0> ; <htcite1>), but additional data is given during 1288 inquiries, according to them, Estevão Peres de Tavares's grandfather owned the region (<htcite2>).

people.name: Pero Viegas
history: When Fray Bernardo de Britto cites Tavares' family, he informs that the lineage possibly had the origin with "Pedro Viegas de Tauares". To support this, he cites a donation to Salzeda in "do anno de mil & duzentos & tres" [the year of 1203] ().

When we talk about Pedro Viegas' parents, there are different proposes, so it is applied the scientific method () to evaluate them:
  • The scientific method starts with the hypotheses:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: has as basis the Fray Bernardo de Britto conclusions. To reach them, it was used patronymic and time that lived Egas, Bufa, and Mor Pais de Curveira (). Some authors also name Egas, Bufa, as Egas Garcia da Fonseca ( ; ), but it is not possible to discard the possibility of his name to be Egas Gomes Barroso ( ; ).
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: made by Manuel Abranches de Soveral, where he would be son of Egas Mendes [de Ribadouro] ().
  • After having the hypotheses, it is necessary to look for primary sources that allow to confirm them:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: When Fray Bernardo de Brito made this hypothesisiis, he said explicitly that this was a possibility "Dom Pedro Viegas podia ser filho de Dom Egas Bufa" (), and he did not give any proof of this relationship.
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: Don Dinis' inquiries informs us that Calvo, Estevão Peres de Tavares' grandfather, owned Tavares region ( ; ). Through Afonso III's inquiries it is known that "Menendus Gunsalvi de Afonsica et Stephanus Petri de Taavares sunt patroni" both Tavares and Fonseca had shared patronate of Santa Maria de Villa Maior ( ; ). The Fonseca's family are possible descendants of Ribadouro's family (). Documentation available in Santa Maria de Salzedas monastery also helps this hypothesis: The monastery was granted to Egas Moniz's widow (). A few years later, his possible grandson, Egas Mendes, confirms a donation to the same monastery (). Considering the patronymic, this Egas Mendes could be Pero Viegas' father, and using the same logic, Estevão Peres de Tavares could be his son, according to Manuel Abranches de Soveral (). Using one more time the patronymic, Egas Mendes could be Gonçalo Viegas' father. Gonçalo Viegas grew up in Fonseca ( ; ), and his children began to use the surname from this region. One of his children, Mem Gonçalves da Fonseca, was already cited in this study (), as a person sharing patronate with his possible cousin and brother-in-law, Estevão Peres de Tavares.
  • The THEORY is made using primary sources and comparing them with the hypotheses:
    • The HYPOTHESIS 1 has some issues:
      1. There is not any documentation to support the relationship between Pero Viegas and Egas, Buffa.
      2. Count D. Pedro informs that Egas, Buffa, had a unique son, that is homonymous of Egas Mendes' possible son, and both did not sign the surname Fonseca (). Probably this homonymous son was the origin of confusion that the owner's of Leomil were the same owner of Fonseca.
      3. The first people known to sign the surname Fonseca were Gonçalves Viegas' children ( ; ). Gonçalo could be a supposed brother of Pero Viegas.
      4. The relationship between Egas, Buffa, to Fonseca's family has another issue: A. A. Fernandes suggested, and José Augusto de S. Pizarro informs that is possible to accept the idea that Egas, Buffa could be Egas Gomes Barroso ().
      The HYPOTHESIS 2, on the other hand, has documentation that allows it to be real, but the studied documentation does not show the relationships explicitly. Considering both hypotheses, it is preferred to accept Manuel Abranches de Soveral's propose. Q.E.D. (Util new documents appear that can change current understanding, because science always advances with data.).

marriages.name: Tereza de Vila Maior
history: The relationship with Teresa de Vila de Maior is cited by Leontina Ventura (<htcite0>).

source.text: dos Santos Rocha, Rui Miguel L. B., 2017

children.name: Estevão Peres de Tavares
history: The documentation used to define his possible ancestors is the basis to accept this relationship. This relationship is treated as hypothetical, because there is not any documentation citing it explicitly.

children.name: Maria Peres de Tavares
history: Old genealogical books inform that she was the sister of Estevão Peres de Tavares (<htcite0> ; <htcite1>). Considering the patronymic and the fact that her husband, Mem Gonçalves da Fonseca, had a shared patronate of Vila Maior with her brother (<htcite2> ; <htcite3>), it is possible she is also the daughter of the suggested couple. Like her supposed brother, this relationship is treated as hypothetical, because there is not any documentation citing it explicitly.

families.name: Tavares - Portalegre
history: Descendants of Gonçalo Esteves de Tavares (<htcite0>) that moved to Portalegre. It is not uncommon books to say that Gonçalo Esteves de Tavares and his descendant were mayor of Portalegre, but documentation gives this title for few of them (<htcite1>).

people.name: Pedro de Tavares
history: Nobleman of infant D. Henrique and major of Portalegre according to chancellery (<htcite0> ; <htcite1>).

The chancellery of Don Afonso V also informs about the dowry given to Pedro de Tavares when he had marriage with Isabel de Sousa (<htcite2> ; <htcite3>). The marriage happened, and it can be proved through the legitimation book of Leitura Nova (<htcite4>).
He was with his [hypothetical] father, Gonçalo de Tavares, during the war in Tânger (<htcite5>).

Pedro de Tavares was the last mayor of Portalegre from Tavares' family. This lost, however, was something not accepted so easily. Fr. Luiz de Sousa describes that before migration to Aveiro, the Tavares made an embarrassment to their king (<htcite6>).

The available documentation always names him Pedro de Tavares, and Alão de Moraes follows them (<htcite7>). On the other hand, Gaio names him in different ways: when he describes the Tavares' family names him "Pedro Esteves Tavares" (<htcite8>), while he cites him as "Pedro Tavares" when working with Sousa's family (<htcite9>).

marriages.name: Isabel de Sousa
history: According to her legitimation (<htcite0>).

source.text: Torre do Tombo, Leitura Nova, Livro 2 de Legitimações

children.name: Gonçalo Tavares de Sousa
history: The relationship is cited during Mira's donation by the king, Don Manuel I (<htcite0>).

source.text: Torre do Tombo, Chancelaria Régia, D. João III, L. 15

children.name: Fernão de Tavares
history: If somehow Gaspar Frutuoso text can be proved, he would be a son of this couple (<htcite0>). See his description for more details.

source.text: Morais, Cristóvão Alão de, 1943-1948, Vol. 3, Tomo 2

people.name: Fernão de Tavares
history: He is not cited by genealogical books, neither it was possible to find any other document citing him. The unique available document is his grandson, Ruy Tavares, coat of arm written on December, 2nd of 1534 that says Ruy Tavares is "filho legítimo que é de Fernando Annes Tavares e neto de Fernão Tavares, de Portalegre, que foi do tronco d'esta geração e fidalgo" [legitimate son of Fernando Annes de Tavares and grandson of Fernão Tavares, from Portalegre, that was part of the trunk of this generation] ().

Gaspar Frutuoso informs "Fernão de Anes Tavares, que era primo-irmão de Simão de Sousa Tavares, filhos de dois irmãos, naurais de Portalegre" [Fernão Anes Tavares, was cousin of Simão de Sousa Tavares, sons of two brothers, both from Portalegre] (). To check this sentence, we are not going to use the scientific method (), because of cited lack of documentation that does not allow neither to confirm nor to deny it, so it is only analyzed if the HYPOTHESIS is acceptable:
  • The surnames given to Simão are not the same used in Don Manuel I chancellery, where he is cited as "Simão Tavares" during a contract of owner over a region () and as "Simão Tavares de Sousa" in a letter (). Anselmo Braamcamp Freire follows chancellery, citing him as "Simão Tavares" (). It is always good to remember, the reader, that surname usage was flexible during a long period of time ( ; ).
  • The "Tenças de D. Manuel I" cites Simão Tavares as a son of Gonçalo Tavares (), the same happens in Dom João III's chancellery ().
  • The proposed generations apparently do not have anachronism:
    • Ruy Tavares had a coat of arms in the year 1534 (), and probably he was already an adult.
    • Using an inventory of year 1605 made in Brazil, that informs Fernão Dias (Paes) had "herdeiros menores de vinte e cinco anos" [heirs younger than twenty-fiver years old] (), so it is assumed approximately that during centuries 16th and 17th, people younger than 25 years old were not considered adult.
    • Let us suppose the coat of arms was given in the same year Ruy was 25 years old, this implies he was born in 1509 (1534 - 25).
    • Analogous math can be done to estimate his father, Fernão Annes de Tavares, birth year that should be around the year of 1484 (1509 - 25).
    • Finally, we use the logic with his grandfather, Fernão de Tavares, and we arrive at the conclusion that he probably was born in the year of 1459 (1459 - 25). This date is compatible with the marriage date between Pedro de Tavares and Isabel de Sousa that probably happened in the year 1450 ( ; ) and the fact that Fernão Tavares was younger than Gonçalo Tavares de Sousa, owner of Mira ().
  • In the coat of arms, Fernão Tavares is mentioned as a nobleman () like Pedro de Tavares ( ; ).
  • All these data show that the hypothesis only needs a document to modify its status as a fact/theory.

families.name: Tavares - Açores
history: Descendants of Fernão Eannes Tavares and Isabel Gonçalves de Moraes, according to Priest Gaspar Frutuoso (<htcite0>).

people.name: Fernão Eannes de Tavares
history: It was not possible to find a primary source different of his son's coat of arm (<a href="#" onclick="htCleanSources(); htFillPrimarySource('a0fb89b0-34b3-485c-bd96-ec5c9b20d922'); return false;">Torre do Tombo, Chancelaria Régia, D. João III, L. 20, fl. 194v (img. 388)), and the document does not cite his [supposed] mother.

When the Priest Gaspar Frutuoso describes him, he informs details about the reason he moved to Madeira Island, leaving Portalegre (). He also cites a [supposedd] marriage with an Isabel Gonçalves de Moraes, but neither him and nor his wife, descendants of noblemen, are cited years later by Henrique Henriques de Noronha (). There was only a citation about an "Isabel Fernandes Tavares filha de Fernão Eanes e Catarina Vaz Tavares" [Isabel Fernandes Tavares daughter of Fernão Eanes and Catarina Vaz Tavares] (), but they do not look like the same.

marriages.name: Isabel Gonçalves de Moraes
history: According to Priest Gaspar Frutuoso (<htcite0>).

children.name: Ruy Tavares
history: According to his son's coat of arm (<a href="#" onclick="htCleanSources(); htFillPrimarySource('a0fb89b0-34b3-485c-bd96-ec5c9b20d922'); return false;">Torre do Tombo, Chancelaria Régia, D. João III, L. 20, fl. 194v (img. 388)).

families.name: Tavares - Queluz de Minas/Lafaiete
history: Descendant of Antonio Tavares de Mello, from Açores, and Antonia Tavares Dinis.

people.name: José Tavares de Mello
history: According to his testament, written on May, 24th of 1791, he was born in "Bom Jesus do Rabo de Peixe, Ilha Sam Miguel, Bispado de Angra" (). He arrived in Brazil before May, 6th of 1760, when he received lands in Minas Gerais ().

The surname flexibility ( ; ) also appears in José Tavares de Mello's family. To confirm this last sentence, it is assumed that José had a long life, and the cited parents in his testament were the couple Manuel Affonso and Guiomar Cabral, that had marriage on August, 15th of 1691 (). This hypothesis is reinforced with:
  • On average, women are pregnant around 23 years () and "fecundity usually ends at around 39–41 years" (). Using these data, if Guiomar Cabral were 22 years old when she had her marriage, she still could be pregnant around the year of 1710 [year of 1692 + 18 years (41 years - 23 years)]. We also remember the reader she could have marriage younger, and as consequence she could be pregnant after 1710.
  • If José Tavares de Mello was born either in the first or in the second decade of the 18th century, he could reach the last decade of the century alive.
  • Guiomar Cabral's father was named Antonio Tavares (), so José Tavares de Mello had his grandfather surname.
  • José Tavares de Mello's [probable] mother, Guiomar Cabral de Mello, also inherits the surname from an ancestor, to be more specific, her great-grandmother, take a look at her ancestors: Guiomar Cabral de Mello, daughter of Bárbara Travassos (), granddaughter of Maria Travassos (), and great-granddaughter of Guiomar Cabral de Melo ( ; ).
We will show in the future, he [probably] is descendant of Fernão de Tavares.

marriages.name: Antonia Tavares Diniz
history: According to her husband's testament (<htcite0>) and her inventory transcribed by Quincas de Almeida (<htcite1>).

families.name: Tavares - São Paulo
history: Descendant of Fernão Vieira Tavares from São Miguel - Azores (<htcite0>), and one of his wives.

people.name: Fernão Vieira Tavares
history: As it was said, he is from São Miguel - Azores (), and he is also presented as "sujeito de conhecida nobreza" [person with known nobility] (), he also worked in noble positions while Sousas' family were owner of São Vicente (), so he could be a descendant of Tavares and Sousas from Portalegre.

In 20th century, Marta M. Amato supposed:
  • "O "Macedo" que aparece em seu nome em inúmeros documentos poderá provir de algum ancestral desconhecido; por exemplo, do seu provável avô, o Mestre de Campo Antônio Raposo Tavares, conhece-se unicamente o nome de seu pai, Fernão Vieira Tavares."

(<htcite3>). She is correct to say that some members of Macedo's family was living in Açores, as Dr. Augusto F. do Amaral shows (<htcite4>), but as it is shown in a descendant diligence, Macedo is the surname of Fernão Vieira Tavares's wife. Marta M. Amato's hypothesis, on the other hand, cannot be completely discarded, because Dr. Augusto F. do Amaral also shows in his work, that there is a historical relantionship between the families Macedo and Morais (<htcite5>), so the Macedo could have some relationship with Isabel Gonçalves de Moraes, Fernão Eanes de Tavares's wife (<htcite6>), or still some ascendendant of Fernão Vieira Tavares could have a marriage with Macedo that lived in Açores.

marriages.name: Francisca Pinheiro de Macedo
history: According to witnesses and a genealogical tree available in her descendant's diligence (<htcite0>).
Her sister had a marriage with a man "Fulano Zurarte", that had fame to be a Jewish person, and this also brought fame to her family, but this fame did not have basis ().

When José G. Salvador cites her, he names her as "Francisca Pinheiro da Costa Bravo" and also adds "também cremos ser de linhagem cristã nova" [we also believe she is from Jewish family] (), but he says this information without to present documentation that proves she signed surname "Costa Bravo". We also highlight that José G. Salvador only says he has "crença" [belief] that she could be a Jewish person.

marriages.name: Maria da Costa
history: According to her own inquisitorial lawsuit (<htcite0>).

source.text: Torre do Tombo, Tribunal do Santo Ofício, Inquisição de Lisboa, proc. 11992, img. 1

children.name: Antonio Raposo Tavares
history: According to different witness and a family tree available in a descendant diligence (<htcite0>).

source.text: Torre do Tombo, Tribunal do Santo Ofício: Conselho Geral, Habilitações, Pedro, mç. 25, doc. 476, img. 27, 62, 93

people.name: Antonio Raposo Tavares
history: António Raposo Tavares first marriage can also be considered an indirect confirmation of his father origin. His wife, Beatriz Bicudo, is daughter of Maria Bicudo (<htcite0>), that is sister of Antonio Bicudo, that informs us their father was born in San Miguel Island (<htcite1>), the same Island that Fernão Vieira de Tavares was born (<htcite2>). In 16th century was not uncommon a marriage by agreement (<htcite3>), so two families from Açores could have a marriage with a goal. After Beatriz Bicudo passed away, Antonio Raposo Tavares had another marriage with Lucrecia Leme (<htcite4>).

It is possible to observe in The São Paulo Town hall documentation, that in the majority of the times, Antonio Raposo Tavares was in highligthed positions, but there was one time that they tried to arrest him by to organize a "amotinamento" [mutiny]:
  • They try to arrest him by "amotinamento" [mutiny] on September, 5th of 1627, but the prisión apparently did not happen ().
  • Between 1629 and 1639, we have the majority of citations to Antonio Raposo Tavares. It is possible to highlighth his actions as an Ordinary Judge and "ouvidor", it is also possible to find his signature ().
  • The last citations to his name, he is cited as a captain ().

The defamation that he received by his aunt to marry with "Fulano Zuarte" (<htcite8>) could also have relationship with civil war between Pires and Camargo, because António Raposo Tavares was son-in-law of Manuel Pires (<htcite9>) and he also had a marriage with  Lucrécia Leme (<htcite10>), both families were enemies of Camargo's family.

During the 20th and 21st centuries, one more time people began to say that Antonio Raposo Tavares would be a Jewish person, but are the sources used to do this affirmations reliable? We are going to use the scientific method () one more time to evaluate them:
  • The scientific method starts with the hypotheses:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: it was made during wartimes, according to it, Antonio Raposo Tavares' mother, Francisca Pinheiro de Macedo, would be a Jewish person.
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: José G. Salvador found the documentation for it, but he did not suggest it (), this was made by Anita Novinsky years later in a paper (), and after this she gave orientation for her student, Marcelo M. A. Bogaciovas, about it ().
    • HYPOTHESIS 3: he is not a Jewish person, this means that both previous hypotheses do not have foundation.
  • After having the hypotheses, it is necessary to look for primary sources that allow to confirm them:
    • HYPOTHESIS 1: this hypothesis does not have any documentation. According to witnesses, it was made when Antonio Raposo Tavares' aunt had a marriage with a "Fulano Zuarte" (). When José Gonçalves Salvador worked with her, he arrived in the belief ["crença"] that she could be a Jewish person (), he also gave surnames that we could not confirm through documentation.
    • HYPOTHESIS 2: José G. Salvador cites the text that Anita Novinsky used years later to create her hypothesis. According to Anita Novinsky: "A declaração de judaísmo de Antonio Raposo Tavares vem expressa numa frase que pronunciou quando, sendo questionado por um jesuíta, sobre a autoridade moral dos paulistas em guerreá-lo, respondeu que com a autoridade "que Deus lhe dava nos livros de Moisés"." [The Jewish declaration from Antonio Raposo Tavares was expressed in a sentence he said while he was asked by a Jesuit about the moral that Paulistas had to fight against them, he answered that authority was given "by God in Moses' books"] (). This sentence can be confirmed through Jesuit Pablo Pastells' work ().
    • HYPOTHESIS 3: In the diligence for familiar of Holy Office of Pedro Dias Paes, distinct witnesses said he was a "cristão-velho" [Christian], and it was also added Antonio Raposo Tavares's granddaughter baptism. People also affirmed that the fame to be Jewish happened when his aunt had a marriage with "Fulano Zuarte" (). More or less 10 years after getting his master degree, in a paper sent to ASBRAP, Marcelo M. A. Bogaciovas affirms "... cuja dissertação continha muitos erros (inclusive de português...).2 Houve tempo hábil de apresentar uma segunda versão para a banca, mas foi a primeira versão que foi disponibilizada na internet. Continuo preparando versão final para publicação em livro..." [the final text had many mistakes (including Portuguese errors ...). 2. There was enough time to present a second version to the revisers, but it was the first version that was published, and it is available on the WEB. I continue preparing final version to publish a book ...] (). And for this specific scenario, the book presents important corrections. The author Marcelo M. A. Bogaciovas dedicates 5 pages of the book, with presentation of documents, to demonstrate that Antonio Raposo Tavares is "cristão-velho" [Christian] ().
  • The THEORY is made using primary sources and comparing them with the hypotheses:
    • The HYPOTHESIS 1 was proved false with different witnesses in a diligence (). The HYPOTHESIS 2 does not appear to have any document to sustain that he is a Jewish person, and the old student, Marcelo M. A. Bogaciovas, fixed everything later. This hypothesis also ignores completely that Holy Office of Lima had collaboration from Spanish Jesuits, to kill Portuguese Catholic people (). This event was observed in different countries, not only in Paraguay. Antonio José Saraiva describes as false charges were made against selected victims (). We end the argumentation remembering the reader that classify people as a Jewish was a vengeance used during wartimes (), and Antonio Raposo Tavares had a stepmother Jewish (), that could teach him the Jewish worldwide. The HYPOTHESIS 3 have distinct witnesses and documentation to sustain it, including a document with Antonio Raposo Tavares' words, added by Marcelo M. A. Bogaciovas, after to be oriented a mistake. With everything presented, we follow Marcelo M. A. Bogaciovas' conclusion, affirming that Antonio Raposo Tavares is a "cristão-velho" [Christian]. Q.E.D. (Util new documents appear that can change current understanding, because science always advances with data.).
